title: Primrose And Violet
content:
Primrose and Violet -
    May they help thee to forget
    All that love should not remember,
    Sweet as meadows after rain
    When the sun has come again,
    As woods awakened from December.
    How they wash the soul from stain!
    How they set the spirit free!
    Take them, dear, and pray for me.
meaning:
Richard Le Gallienne's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Primrose And Violet"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
